title: Part 2: Rescale to Float + Normalize with Broadcasting

cues.label: Input
cues.headline: Pixels arrive as integers 0-255; models want floats
cues.narration: Pixels arrive as uint8 integers from 0 to 255, but models need floats — dividing a uint8 array by 255 gives correct values in 0 to 1 but as float64, which wastes twice the memory, so call astype(float32) before dividing.

cues.label: Transform
cues.headline: astype(float32)/255, then (arr - mean)/std
cues.narration: arr.astype(np.float32)/255 maps 0-255 into 0-1; then (arr - mean)/std applies the length-3 ImageNet mean and std, broadcast across every pixel, to center each channel near zero.

cues.label: Handoff
cues.headline: A float32 array, roughly zero-mean per channel
cues.narration: The result is a float32 array, still shaped (896, 896, 3) but now roughly zero-mean per channel: the shape is unchanged while the dtype and range have changed.
